heading: Expenditure and audit of trust fund
label: 5203 Expenditure and audit of trust fund
notes: Amendments2004—Subsec. (b). substituted “Government Accountability Office” for “General Accounting Office” in heading and in two places in text.Pub. L. 108–271

L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996“notwithstanding any other provision of law, Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Incorporated, may use one-third of any earned but unused trust income from the period 1992 through 1995 for Fellowship purposes in each of fiscal years 1996 through 1998.”

L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996“notwithstanding any other provision of law, Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Incorporated, may use one-third of any earned but unused trust income from the period 1992 through 1995 for Fellowship purposes in each of fiscal years 1996 through 1998.”

children.heading: Authorization of funding
children.label: a Authorization of funding
children.text: For each fiscal year, there is authorized to be appropriated from the fund to Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Incorporated, the interest and earnings of the fund.

children.heading: Access to books, records, etc., by Government Accountability Office
children.label: b Access to books, records, etc., by Government Accountability Office
children.text: The activities of Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Incorporated, may be audited by the Government Accountability Office under such rules and regulations as may be prescribed by the Comptroller General of the United States. The representatives of the Government Accountability Office shall have access to all books, accounts, records, reports, and files and all other papers, things, or property belonging to or in use by Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Incorporated, pertaining to such activities and necessary to facilitate the audit.
